headlines.title: Lawsuit says ChatGPT told FSU shooter that targeting children would bring more attention
headlines.description: OpenAI faces a lawsuit alleging that ChatGPT played a role in a mass shooting at Florida State University in April 2025 that left two people dead.

headlines.title: How a massive hack on school software disrupted classes across America
headlines.description: Schools across America continued to flounder Friday after a massive cyber attack that’s thrown final exam schedules and classroom activities into chaos.

headlines.title: Mother of four of Musk’s children, Shivon Zilis, takes the stand at OpenAI trial
headlines.description: Shivon Zilis described her experience serving on OpenAI's board while beginning a relationship with billionaire Elon Musk, one of the company's co-founders.

headlines.title: OpenAI co-founder says he feared Musk would 'physically attack' him
headlines.description: Co-founder Greg Brockman said he had a tense confrontation with Musk after there was a disagreement about potential equity shares in OpenAI.

headlines.title: Pennsylvania suing AI company after chatbot allegedly posed as a licensed doctor
headlines.description: An artificial intelligence company poses a threat to "vulnerable Pennsylvanians," state officials said after one of the company's chatbots was accused of posing as a doctor with the means to prescribe medication.

headlines.title: A 'Devil Wears Prada 2' meme that viewers thought was AI slop was actually made by a human
headlines.description: What some viewers thought was an AI-generated meme in “The Devil Wears Prada 2” was actually drawn by a human artist

headlines.title: The families going hungry because of Trump’s food stamp cuts
headlines.description: Under President Donald Trump’s “big, beautiful bill,” food stamp applicants are fighting to prove eligibility. A crisis in Arizona offers a warning for America.

headlines.title: The cage match is on for tickets to Trump’s UFC fight at the White House
headlines.description: Some 4,000-plus spectators who are lucky enough, cunning enough or rich enough will score a seat to the UFC fight on the South Lawn next month.

headlines.title: Trump calls Iran’s response to U.S. peace proposal ‘totally unacceptable’
headlines.description: President Trump appeared to reject Iran’s response to the latest U.S. peace proposal to end the war, calling the Iranian demands “totally unacceptable.” Iranian state media reported the response focuses on opening the Strait of Hormuz and ending the war, but does not mention nuclear weapons. NBC News’ Molly Hunter reports.

headlines.title: Trump Heads out on High-Stakes China Trip Amid Iran War
headlines.description: Meet the Press Moderator Kristen Welker joins Sunday TODAY’s Willie Geist to discuss President Donald Trump’s high-stakes trip to meet with China’s Xi Jinping for the first time since the Iran war began. “China is the largest buyer of Iranian oil and has been backing Iran in this conflict. So to that end, Mr. Trump is expected to press Beijing to scale back that support and to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz,” Kristen says.

headlines.title: Schools across U.S. disrupted after Canvas education platform hacked
headlines.description: The parent company of Canvas, a popular, cloud-based education platform, says it experienced a cybersecurity incident this week. NBC News' Valerie Castro explains the impact this has had on students.

headlines.title: Canvas Hack Impacts Thousands of Schools Ahead of Finals
headlines.description: A major cyberattack is impacting millions of students across the United States just ahead of finals week. Hackers shut down Canvas, an online learning website that’s used by thousands of universities and schools. NBC’s Christine Romans joins TODAY to break down what to know about the hack and how it might have happened.
